name: Interval-Based Reclamation (IBR)
summary: Wen et al. (PPoPP 2018): threads reserve a finite epoch interval; blocks track birth/retire epoch; a block is freeable when no reservation interval intersects its lifetime. TagIBR / 2GEIBR / POIBR variants trade throughput for space; avoids per-dereference fence; bounded space like HP. Rochester library; basis for many subsequent SMR comparisons.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
Wen et al.'s **Interval-Based Reclamation** (PPoPP 2018) generalises Hazard Eras: threads reserve a finite epoch interval; blocks track birth and retire epoch; a block is freeable when no thread's reservation interval intersects the block's lifetime interval.

The paper introduces several variants — **TagIBR** (tag-and-interval), **2GEIBR** (two-generation), **POIBR** (per-object intervals) — trading throughput for space. All share two key properties: avoids the per-dereference fence required by hazard pointers, and bounds memory like HP rather than EBR.

The University of Rochester's **Interval-Based Reclamation library** is the reference implementation and has become the basis for many subsequent SMR comparisons (Hyaline, NBR, VBR all benchmark against it).
